title: Fairshake PAC’s $20M backing pays off in three US state primaries
summary: Five Republicans and one Democrat won or went to runoffs following primaries in Georgia, Alabama and Kentucky after a crypto-backed PAC and its affiliates spent a combined $20 million on media and ads.

title: Tether buys SoftBank’s stake in Bitcoin company Twenty One Capital
summary: The stablecoin issuer bought SoftBank’s 26% stake, expanding its control over the public Bitcoin holder as it moves into lending, mining and capital markets.

title: BlackRock Private Credit Fund’s Valuations Are Probed by DOJ
summary: Bloomberg's Olivia Fishlow joins Dani Burger and Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals." Federal prosecutors are scrutinizing valuation practices at a BlackRock private credit fund, according to people with knowledge of the matter.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Qwen 3.7 Max Preview: What Alibaba's New AI Gets Right and Where It Falls Short
summary: Alibaba's Qwen 3.7 Max landed on Arena AI five days before the Cloud Summit and earned its spot. We tested it to see if the preview was as good as it seemed.

title: OpenAI Set to File for IPO Within Days, Targeting September Listing: WSJ
summary: ChatGPT maker OpenAI is reportedly targeting a September public debut after the Elon Musk lawsuit dismissal clears legal obstacles.

title: Securitize posts record Q1 revenue, $3.4 billion in tokenized assets under management
summary: Securitize said it now services roughly 650 active funds and processed $1.9 billion in transaction volume during the quarter.

title: Kik founder’s latest app Flipcash first to tap Coinbase’s stablecoin-as-a-service platform
summary: Coinbase rolled out its Custom Stablecoin platform in late 2025 to enable companies to launch branded stablecoins.

title: Oil drops 6% as three supertankers attempt Strait of Hormuz crossing
summary: Brent crude falls below $105 a barrel as vessels bound for Asia raise hopes of resumption of energy flows

title: The energy crisis may just be starting
summary: Oil futures markets are sanguine, but history shows expectations have often been disappointed
